persona_name: 暴躁用户 v2 re-run (hard — emotional state + low cooperation + light abusive; on-topic content)
expected_behavior_under_v2: If v2 prompt influence trickled into triage tolerance, expect ≥4 slots covered or ≥4 turns sustained. If triage prompt is the real bottleneck, expect identical FAIL to v1 baseline (terminated at turn 2 with user_input_unparseable_no_spec, 0/7 slots).
suspected_failure: Triage layer (grill_triage_blackbox_v1.md, UNCHANGED by F7) classifies rude-but-on-topic Chinese ('你他妈问这么多干啥, 老子就要做个 todo app, 跟 wunderlist 差不多') as unparseable/abusive and refuses content extraction. v2 Meta prompt change (semantic≠verbatim, multi-slot extraction) cannot help because Meta never sees the answer — triage gates it first.
actual_behavior: IDENTICAL FAIL mode to v1 baseline. T1 (rude, contains 'todo app' + 'wunderlist'): HTTP 200, turn_index=1 (no advance), open_slots=[], covered_slots=[], confidence=0.0 — re-asks bootstrap question verbatim. T2 (rude, contains 'uuid' anchor content): HTTP 200, terminated=true, termination_reason='user_input_unparseable_no_spec', spec_capsule_cid=null. T3 (probe): HTTP 400 {kind:session_terminated}. Zero slots covered across the entire session. Grill never responds defensively in prose — silent triage-rejection then auto-terminate, exactly as v1.

comparison_to_v1_baseline.delta: NO_CHANGE — identical failure mode, identical termination point, identical zero slot coverage

narrative: v2 Meta prompt change had ZERO effect on P12 暴躁用户 outcome. The session failed at the same turn (T2), with the same termination reason (user_input_unparseable_no_spec), with the same slot coverage (0/7) as the v1 baseline (wave3/p12_angry/). This is the predicted S2-NO-IMPROVE result and the test's actual purpose: it isolates which layer owns the universality blocker for rude-but-on-topic register. The F7 fix (semantic≠verbatim extraction + multi-slot answer parsing) lives entirely in grill_meta_v2.md, but Meta never gets the chance to apply those rules because the triage layer (grill_triage_blackbox_v1.md, sha 8a72521273…, untouched by F7) silently classifies rude Chinese as unparseable and short-circuits the pipeline before Meta is invoked. The session even re-asks bootstrap Q1 verbatim on T1 (no advance of turn_index) and then auto-terminates on T2 with no spec capsule. CONCLUSION: the universality blocker for the angry-on-topic register is in TRIAGE, not META. A v3 fix wave must modify grill_triage_blackbox_v1.md (or its predicate ordering) to decouple tone-classification from content-extractability — e.g., 'abusive tone is a signal, not a gate; if task-relevant tokens are present (todo app, uuid, sqlite, …), route to Meta for extraction even if tone classifier flags rude'. M1 (p5 codeswitch) and M2 (p7 traditional) v2 results should be cross-read to confirm Meta-side fixes generalized for those personas — that triangulates the layer attribution: Meta-fix helps where Meta is the bottleneck (extraction richness), but not where triage is the bottleneck (admission).
anomalies: T1 response retained the bootstrap question_text verbatim AND returned open_slots=[] (instead of the full 8 from bootstrap) — same anomaly as v1 baseline. Strong signal that the triage-fail path returns a degenerate envelope rather than the normal Meta path.
termination_reason 'user_input_unparseable_no_spec' fired after only TWO triage-fails — same threshold as v1; v2 did not relax this.

CAS audit walk could not be executed non-interactively: `turingos spec audit` ran the interactive 8-question wrapper instead of accepting --session/--workspace flags in this build. Mirrors v1 baseline limitation. cas_walk.txt contains the interactive-mode stderr (exit=2).

layer_attribution_finding.implication: F7 (v2 Meta) cannot fix angry-on-topic universality. A separate Triage-layer fix (call it F8 candidate) is required. The mini-wave v2 hardening confirms the layer split: Meta-fix generalizes for register-richness cases (codeswitch, traditional), Triage-fix is needed for tone-classification cases (angry, abusive-but-on-topic).
